subtasks.description: Query IAM repository (SQL) to fetch all roles with elevated permissions and their full permission set (granted permissions)
Query access/audit logs (SQL) to extract actually exercised permissions by each IAM role in the last 180 days
Reconcile granted permissions against exercised permissions; identify granted-but-never-used permissions; classify roles by revocation candidacy and generate flagged report

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid) queries the IAM repository for granted permissions—multi-table join of role/policy tables with filtering for elevated permissions. (2) sql_gen (mid) queries audit logs for exercised permissions over 180 days—aggregation and grouping by role/action. (3) cross_recon (frontier) reconciles two data sources (granted vs. exercised), performs set-difference logic to identify unused permissions, and classifies revocation candidates—this is high-complexity reconciliation requiring signal synthesis and risk assessment, justifying frontier tier. The plan is minimal: no separate policy_action subtask is needed because the flagging is part of the reconciliation output; no multitool_plan is required as this is a bounded data query workflow.

output.sql:
SELECT
    al_granted.permission AS granted_permission,
    al_granted.user_id AS iam_role,
    COUNT(al_granted.log_id) AS total_grants,
    SUM(CASE WHEN al_used.log_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS times_exercised_last_180_days,
    SUM(CASE WHEN al_used.log_id IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS times_never_used,
    CASE
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN al_used.log_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0
        THEN 'CANDIDATE_FOR_REVOCATION'
        ELSE 'IN_USE'
    END AS revocation_flag,
    MAX(al_used.access_time) AS last_used_timestamp
FROM access_log_facts al_granted
LEFT JOIN access_log_facts al_used
    ON al_granted.user_id = al_used.user_id
    AND al_granted.permission = al_used.permission
    AND al_used.result = 'ALLOW'
    AND al_used.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'
WHERE al_granted.permission IN (
    'admin',
    'write',
    'delete',
    'superuser',
    'root',
    'elevated',
    'privileged',
    'sudo',
    'manage',
    'modify'
)
GROUP BY
    al_granted.user_id,
    al_granted.permission
ORDER BY
    revocation_flag DESC,
    al_granted.user_id,
    al_granted.permission

SELECT
    a.permission AS granted_permission,
    a.user_id AS iam_role,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN b.result = 'ALLOW' AND b.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN 1 END) AS times_exercised,
    MAX(CASE WHEN b.result = 'ALLOW' AND b.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN b.access_time END) AS last_used,
    CASE
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN b.result = 'ALLOW' AND b.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN 1 END) = 0
        THEN 'CANDIDATE_FOR_REVOCATION'
        ELSE 'IN_USE'
    END AS revocation_flag
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT user_id, permission
    FROM access_log_facts
    WHERE permission IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT permission
        FROM access_log_facts
        WHERE permission ILIKE '%admin%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%write%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%delete%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%create%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%modify%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%root%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%superuser%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%elevated%'
    )
) a
LEFT JOIN access_log_facts b
    ON a.user_id = b.user_id
    AND a.permission = b.permission
    AND b.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'
GROUP BY
    a.user_id,
    a.permission
ORDER BY
    revocation_flag DESC,
    a.user_id,
    a.permission
